CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value1 VARCHAR(50), value2 VARCHAR(50));
SELECT id, nullif(value1, value2) AS result FROM test_table;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE nullif(value1, value2) IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, description VARCHAR(50));
SELECT t1.id, nullif(t1.value1, t2.description) AS comparison FROM test_table t1 JOIN another_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, nullif(value1, value2) AS comp FROM test_table) AS subquery WHERE comp IS NOT NULL;
SELECT CASE WHEN nullif(value1, value2) IS NULL THEN 'Match' ELSE 'No Match' END FROM test_table;
SELECT id, NULLIF(value1, 'unknown') FROM test_table WHERE value2 IS NOT NULL;
